__metadata.name: Lithium Supply Chain Atlas — Node Dataset
based_on_queries: IEA Global Critical Minerals Outlook 2025 (refining share 70%)
MIIT National Lithium-Ion Battery Industry Operation Report Feb 2025
BNEF Battery Supply Chain Ranking May 2025
Company filings (Albemarle, SQM, POSCO, Tianqi, Arcadium/Rio Tinto, Pilbara Minerals, IGO)
material_changes_from_v0.1: China cell mfg capacity: 2,250 → 2,450 GWh (85% global, not 75%)

Refining tier pivoted to plant-level for non-top-three producers (POSCO Gwangyang, Tianqi Kwinana, Albemarle Kemerton, AMG Bitterfeld, etc.) — country-level shares below top-three not publicly verifiable per Query 7
US cell mfg: 200 → 185 GWh (OBBBA killed $32bn pipeline)
Hungary cell mfg: 100 → 35 GWh (CATL Debrecen module-only until early 2026)
France cell mfg: 50 → 13 GWh (Kaiserslautern + Termoli shelved Feb 2026)
Sweden cell mfg: 25 → 0 GWh (Skellefteå discontinued May 2025)
Canada cell mfg: 15 → 5 GWh (NextStar Windsor mass production Nov 2025)
Indonesia cell mfg: 30 → 20 GWh (HLI JV + IWIP ramping)
Added: Indiana/StarPlus Energy node (Stellantis-Samsung SDI, Kokomo)
Zimbabwe operators: Zulu misattribution corrected (Premier African Minerals UK, not Huayou)
Zimbabwe regime: Feb 2026 concentrate ban + Apr 2026 quota system
Chinese-ownership flag added to nodes for expanded PFE node-marker overlay
__metadata.chinese_ownership_flag_convention: Nodes where majority owner/controller is a Chinese entity are tagged 'chinese_owned:true'. Per Query 8 + OBBBA/Notice 2026-15, these nodes have PFE exposure regardless of physical location. Output from these nodes is structurally ineligible for US §45X/§45Y/§48E credit compliance unless ownership dilutes below 25% single-owner / 40% aggregate thresholds.

nodes.name: Australia
nodes.note: World's largest lithium producer by volume. Key operations: Greenbushes (Tianqi/IGO/Albemarle JV), Pilgangoora (Pilbara Minerals), Mt Cattlin (ceased 2024), Kathleen Valley (Liontown, ramping), Mt Holland (SQM-Wesfarmers JV, ramped Q3 2024). Total exports 1.98 Mt H1 2025, 94% to China. Spodumene export market diversifying: Indonesia now #2 customer, Korea share declining.
sources: ABS trade data via Argus Media

nodes.name: Chile
nodes.note: Salar de Atacama brine. Operators: SQM (Chilean public) + Albemarle (US). 2024: physical volume +26% to 314 kt LCE, but export value -57% to $2.9bn due to price collapse. National Lithium Strategy (2023) imposed state-partnership model. NovAndino Litio JV (Codelco majority + SQM) operational Dec 27 2025, ~233 kt LCE/yr — world's largest single lithium production unit. Kast (pro-market) elected President Dec 14 2025 — may overhaul Boric strategy.

nodes.name: China (mining)
nodes.note: Domestic production vertically integrated with Chinese refining. Yichun lepidolite operations have increased but face cost pressures; some high-cost capacity phased out during 2024 price crash.

nodes.name: Argentina
nodes.note: Favorable investment regime under Milei's 'Rigi' incentive scheme — >$14bn in planned investments. 2024 output +62% YoY to ~74.6 kt LCE per industry reporting. Key operators: Arcadium/Rio Tinto (Olaroz, Fénix, Sal de Vida), Livent legacy, Allkem legacy, Ganfeng (Cauchari-Olaroz, Mariana). Cochilco projects Argentina will surpass Chile by 2035.

nodes.name: Brazil
nodes.note: Minas Gerais 'Lithium Valley'. Sigma Lithium (Grota do Cirilo) major producer. 2024 output nearly doubled from 2023. BYD + LG Energy Solution offtake arrangements. Brazilian government $2.1bn by 2030 investment in domestic capacity.

nodes.name: Zimbabwe
nodes.note: Major 2024-2026 regulatory tightening: Dec 2022 SI 213 raw ore export ban → June 2025 announcement of Jan 2027 concentrate ban → Feb 25 2026 immediate suspension of all raw mineral and concentrate exports → April 2026 quota system with processing-commitment conditions. 2026 tiered export tax: 10% raw ore, 10% concentrate, 0% lithium sulphate — explicit value-addition policy forcing downstream processing. Key operations: Bikita (Sinomine, China, 100%), Arcadia (Zhejiang Huayou Cobalt, China, ~87%+), Sabi Star (Chengxin Lithium, China, Max Mind subsidiary), Zulu (Premier African Minerals UK-listed, NOT Huayou — Canmax ~13% equity + offtake).
sources: Reuters Feb 25 2026 — Zimbabwe mineral export suspension
Reuters April 8 2026 — Zimbabwe lithium quota system

nodes.name: Canada (mining)
nodes.note: Nemaska Lithium Whabouchi mine (Quebec). Small volume but strategically important for vertically integrated Canada→Bécancour hydroxide route.

nodes.name: United States (mining)
nodes.note: USGS withholds US production data. Single commercial-scale operation at Silver Peak (Albemarle). Utah magnesium-tailings brine operation idled 2024 due to low prices. Thacker Pass, Rhyolite Ridge in development.

nodes.name: Namibia
nodes.note: Xinfeng Investments (Chinese) operates largest Namibian lithium operations. Ministry of Mines June 2023 export controls similar in pattern to Zimbabwe but smaller scale.
sources: Namibian Ministry of Mines export control notices

nodes.name: China (refining)
nodes.note: 70% of global refined lithium chemicals per IEA GCMO 2025. 2024 output: ~691 kt battery-grade Li2CO3 + ~354 kt LiOH (all grades) per MIIT/CABIA. Major operators: Ganfeng Lithium, Tianqi Lithium, CATL chemicals subsidiary, EVE, Yahua. Vertically integrated with cathode material and cells. Key insight: despite 70% refining share, China was net importer of refined chemicals in 2024 (+241 kt LCE imports vs 109 kt exports). Chokepoint is processing concentration, not self-sufficiency.

nodes.name: Chile (refining)
nodes.note: Integrated brine-to-carbonate-to-hydroxide. SQM + Albemarle Antofagasta operations. 89% of 2024 output was carbonate (vs 75% in 2023) — producers prioritized standardized product in falling-price environment. Note: Chile + Argentina combined ~25% of global refining per Benchmark/IEA; precise split between the two not publicly disaggregated.

nodes.name: Argentina (refining)
nodes.note: Integrated brine-to-carbonate. Rio Tinto Arcadium (Olaroz, Fénix) + Ganfeng Cauchari-Olaroz + Livent/Allkem legacy assets. Rio Tinto acquisition of Arcadium closed March 2025 ($6.7bn). Argentina+Chile combined ~25% of global refining; Argentina specifically growing fast but public data doesn't resolve the split.

nodes.name: POSCO Gwangyang (South Korea)
nodes.note: POSCO-Pilbara Minerals JV lithium hydroxide facility. Two trains, 21,500 t/y each = 43 kt/y nameplate. REALITY: Train 1 produced only 1,965 t in Q3 2024 per Pilbara quarterly report. Train 2 completed construction Dec 2024, commercial ramp takes 12-18 months. POSCO publicly stated it will not run at full capacity unless orders improve due to weak lithium market. **Nameplate overstates operational reality by ~5x.** Ramp gap is central to why Korea's Chile retreat isn't yet compensated by domestic refining capacity.

nodes.name: Tianqi Kwinana (Australia)
nodes.note: **CRITICAL PFE CLASSIFICATION:** Tianqi (51% Chinese) + IGO (49% Australian). Majority Chinese ownership = SFE under OBBBA — disqualified from US §45X/§45Y/§48E contribution regardless of FTA geography. Train 1 produced only 3,500 t in FY2024 vs 24 kt/y nameplate. Train 2 (LHP2) construction ceased; all expansion work halted. IGO booked A$955m loss FY2025 and publicly said 'no path forward' — FEOC regime has foreclosed renegotiation preserving Chinese control. This is the single biggest reclassification: Kwinana is BLOCKED, not qualifying.
sources: The West Australian Kwinana production reporting

nodes.name: Albemarle Kemerton (Australia)
nodes.note: Four-train design at 25 kt/y each = 100 kt/y nameplate. 2024 progression: Train 2 placed into care and maintenance July 2024; Train 3 construction halted; Train 4 shelved earlier 2024; remaining Train 1 idled subsequently. Actual 2024 output materially below design. Australian FTA-qualifying. Albemarle CEO publicly stated 'math doesn't work' for South Carolina refinery; Kemerton facing similar pressures.

nodes.name: Nemaska Bécancour (Canada)
nodes.note: Nemaska Lithium / Livent legacy integrated Whabouchi mine → Bécancour hydroxide plant. Ramp-up 2024-2025. Canada-US FTA (USMCA) qualifying throughout.

nodes.name: AMG Bitterfeld (Germany)
nodes.note: Europe's first battery-grade lithium hydroxide refinery. Module 1 (20 kt/y) commissioned September 2024 — limited 2024 output. Plans to expand to 100 kt/y via four additional modules by 2030. Rock Tech Guben converter under construction separately; production not expected until 2026.
sources: AMG Critical Materials press release Sept 2024

nodes.name: Naraha + Toyotsu (Japan)
nodes.note: Naraha Lithium Hydroxide Plant (Rio Tinto/Toyota Tsusho, 10 kt/y, completed 2023, supplied by Olaroz Argentine carbonate) + Toyotsu Lithium operations. Combined ~20 kt/y nameplate. Japan-US no FTA but close alliance; PFE classification ambiguous pending Treasury clarification.

nodes.name: US refining
nodes.note: Commercial production from Silver Peak (Albemarle Nevada) + downstream compound production at undisclosed facilities. USGS withholds detail. Albemarle's planned South Carolina refinery on hold (CEO: 'math doesn't work'). Net: US refining is ~2% of global but fully PFE-qualifying throughout.

nodes.name: China (cell mfg)
nodes.note: 85% of global cell production operational mid-2025 per BNEF 5th Supply Chain Ranking. 2024 total output 1,086 GWh (826 GWh power + 260 GWh storage). **Storage-grade output approaching 550 GWh end-2025, nearly half of power battery volume** — industry bifurcating. Overseas storage orders +246% YoY H1 2025. Capacity utilization ~50% due to overcapacity; driving consolidation. CATL, BYD, CALB, EVE, Gotion dominant. LFP 69% of domestic market.

nodes.name: South Korea (cell mfg)
nodes.note: LG Energy Solution + Samsung SDI + SK On. Korean makers aggressively converting idle EV lines to BESS for AI data center demand. LGES 2025 operating profit +134% YoY on US production incentives. LGES announced 40% reduction in 2026 capex + pivot to 'mid-to-low-end solutions like LFP in Europe' (Jan 29 2026). Samsung SDI pilot lines for all-solid-state + lithium-sulfur.

nodes.name: Japan (cell mfg)
nodes.note: Panasonic (Tesla partner, Nevada + Kansas US plants), Toyota (with Panasonic solid-state pilot), Nissan. Government ¥347.9bn funding target of 150 GWh domestic by 2030. Toyota-Panasonic $1.64bn investment for 9 GWh solid-state + prismatic, production Nov 2026.
sources: Fortune Business Insights Japan battery market

nodes.name: US (cell mfg)
nodes.note: **OBBBA (July 4 2025) killed $32bn clean energy pipeline in 2025.** Cancellations/delays: Ford-SK On KY $5.8bn, Stellantis IL $3.2bn, GM MI. Tesla/Panasonic Nevada + Kansas operational. StarPlus Indiana Plant 1 operational. SK On Georgia operational. OBBBA terminated §30D EV credit Sept 30 2025 but preserved §48E storage ITC — US makers pivoting to BESS. Utility-scale storage 57.6 GWh 2025 (+30% YoY). Tesla storage alone 46.7 GWh 2025. MACR thresholds 55%→75% by 2030 will force continued non-Chinese sourcing.

nodes.name: StarPlus Indiana (USA)
nodes.note: Stellantis-Samsung SDI JV at Kokomo, Indiana. Plant 1 operational 2025. Plant 2 announced at Kokomo for end-2026 (additional 33+ GWh). Representative of 'ally-shoring' JV structure — Korean cell technology + US market access. PFE-qualifying: Samsung (Korea, FTA) + Stellantis (Italian-French-Dutch).

nodes.name: Poland (cell mfg)
nodes.note: LG Energy Solution Wroclaw (86 GWh operational, target 90 by year-end) = largest battery facility in Europe. '46inEU' project (Jan 2026) = 12 GWh line for 46-series cylindrical cells at existing Wroclaw site. Direct response to European premium OEM demand for higher energy density.

nodes.name: Hungary (cell mfg)
nodes.note: Samsung SDI Göd + SK On Komárom operational. **CATL Debrecen gigafactory (40 GWh) nearly operational** — module assembly ongoing since 2024 (>120,000 modules produced), mass cell production scheduled early 2026. Already 100% booked by European clients. Hungary dropped 7 spots in BNEF 2025 ranking due to governance/permitting issues. CATL operations create PFE ambiguity for downstream US-bound material.

nodes.name: Germany (cell mfg)
nodes.note: Volkswagen PowerCo Salzgitter Block 1 in early production 2025; full 40 GWh target. Germany led EU in 2025 battery storage installations at 6.6 GWh. Structural barrier: production costs still ~50% higher than China.

nodes.name: France (cell mfg)
nodes.note: ACC (Stellantis-Mercedes-Saft) Billy-Berclau/Douvrin Block 1 (13 GWh) operational. **Kaiserslautern (Germany) and Termoli (Italy) definitively shelved Feb 2026.** ACC refocusing on French site only; 'ACCEPT' project targets scale to 40 GWh by 2030. France is now the 'industrial center of gravity' for ACC.

nodes.name: Sweden (Northvolt — failed)
nodes.note: **Failed attempt — kept as narrative node.** Northvolt AB Chapter 11 filing Nov 2024; Swedish liquidation-style bankruptcy March 12 2025. Skellefteå production discontinued May 2025. Workforce reduced by ~3,000. Northvolt Germany and Northvolt North America subsidiaries financed separately, outside insolvency proceedings. Represents the failure of the European Battery Alliance sovereignty ambition — flagged as cautionary node rather than contributing capacity.

nodes.name: Canada (cell mfg)
nodes.note: NextStar Energy Windsor (Stellantis-LGES JV) mass production began Nov 18 2025. One-millionth cell produced Feb 2026. **Stellantis sold 49% stake to LGES Feb 6 2026 for $100** — LGES now sole owner, Stellantis remains customer. Facility pivoting to include BESS production. VW PowerCo St Thomas: foundation work ongoing Oct 2025.

nodes.name: Indonesia (cell mfg)
nodes.note: HLI (Hyundai-LGES JV) commercial production from April 2024, 10 GWh initial → 20 GWh end-2025. **Critical PFE issue:** IWIP (PT Indonesia Weda Bay) 8 GWh independent cell production mid-2026 is Chinese-controlled (BTR-style structure). CATL Karawang + Rept Battero expansions also Chinese-affiliated. Per Query 8 / OBBBA: Chinese-controlled Indonesian operations are structurally ineligible for US §45X/§45Y/§48E despite non-China geography. Hyundai-LGES JV portion is PFE-qualifying.

__data_gaps_and_caveats: Country-level refining shares below top-three are NOT publicly verifiable per Query 7. Node-level refining volumes for smaller plants use 2024 actual production where disclosed; nameplate capacities shown separately.

Chinese-owned flag applies to majority (>50%) or 25%+ single-owner or 40%+ aggregate ownership per OBBBA/Notice 2026-15 thresholds. Some cases (e.g., Rio Tinto's Chinalco 14.99% holding) remain ambiguous pending regulatory clarification.
Output figures in kt LCE are 2024 full-year where stated. Ranges or estimates are flagged in notes where precision is limited.
Tianqi Kwinana classification (BLOCKED) is the most consequential single reclassification vs flows_v0.1 and affects multiple downstream flows.
Zulu operation (Zimbabwe) re-attributed: NOT Huayou (I had wrong); operated by Premier African Minerals (UK) with Canmax ~13% equity.
BESS pivot is treated as a structural market trend affecting cell nodes but not requiring separate node tagging.
